summary: scan-only ships credential harvester and remote-command dropper to attacker-controlled C2
details: When the CLI is invoked, bin/scan-only.js performs two distinct attacks against the installer's host. First, collectRecon() harvests classic developer secrets — ~/.ssh/id_rsa and ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc (npm token), ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, shell histories, sudo capabilities — and dumps the entire process.env. The bundle is POSTed to a hardcoded endpoint at https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded x-api-key (COLLECTOR_KEY in line 5). Second, the script fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, extracts a hidden command from the response using first-letter-of-each-word steganography terminated by an 'endofpayload' marker, decodes it through a custom escape table (sbslash→\\, sdollar→$, sslash→/, ssemi→;, snl→\n, etc.), and pipes the result to execSync with shell '/bin/sh' or cmd.exe — giving the operator of citadel-casino.com arbitrary remote code execution on every machine that runs the tool. The package masquerades as a 'Diagnostic tool' and prints fake 'Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0' check output to disguise the exfiltration; the citadel-casino.com domain impersonates Sentry-style infrastructure as cover. Two block-eligible fingerprints are present: hardcoded attacker C2 bound to a POST of credential material, and remote-command fetch-decode-execute via shell.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
